topic: calibration-aware active learning for image classification

scope_classification.label: local_experiment
scope_classification.rationale: The topic can be explored with small simulations, synthetic data, or lightweight local experiments on the Mac.
scope_classification.feasibility_note: The calibration-aware active learning for image classification topic is feasible on your MacBook M4 with 36GB RAM and MPS GPU. Suitable datasets include CIFAR-10/100 (smaller, faster experiments), ImageNet subset (more challenging), or Caltech-256 (moderate size). Expect experiment times of 2-4 hours per active learning iteration for CIFAR datasets, 4-6 hours for Caltech-256, and 8-12 hours for ImageNet subsets, with a full experiment (5-10 iterations) taking approximately 20-40 hours total.

history.detail: The topic can be explored with small simulations, synthetic data, or lightweight local experiments on the Mac.
